content.text: Imagine conducting ambient music in mid-air. It's like Minority Report, but instead of solving crimes you’re just gently adjusting your nervous system.
For #FigmaMakeathon I built Airwwave (AR) in #FigmaMake: a fullscreen performance instrument where gestures become live ambient soundscapes. It’s built to feel like conducting, not clicking.
Video is long because it’s a performance piece. I’m still hunting bugs, improving responsiveness, and cross browser performance until deadline.
Features: 28 ambient loops (hosted on Cloudinary), 12s fade in/out, 7-step LCD onboarding with 0–100% hold bar, 2-hand tracking + skeleton, 28 pads.
Gestures/FX: select + trigger, speed, reverb, octave, lock, stereo width + pan, tremolo/echo motion, filter resonance, volume shaping.

webPageMetadata.title: I built a drum machine with no audio files. It exports your beat, here's how: For the Figma Makeathon at Contra, I built: UnBeatable, an AI-powered drum machine prototype using Figma Make. A… | Ismel Figueroa
webPageMetadata.description:
I built a drum machine with no audio files. It exports your beat, here’s how: For the Figma Makeathon at Contra, I built: UnBeatable, an AI-powered drum machine prototype using Figma Make. A working music creation tool. Here is what makes it different from every other “interactive prototype” you have seen. Most prototypes stop at the interaction. Tap a button. Something happens. You leave with nothing. UnBeatable changes that...you leave with your downloadable beat. Here’s 7 things I built into his project: - Fully synthesized sound engine, no audio files.
- Glitch-free playback A dual setInterval and request
- Audio export Render your pattern and export your beat.
- AI beat generation using Claude/Supabase and API Credits.
- Full dark and light mode with smooth 0.25s palette transitions.
- 9 genre presets like Rock, Hip Hop, Electronic, Reggae, Latin, etc.
- Every control a real musician would expect, built into the prototype layer. Most designers prototype to show a concept. The best designers prototype to ship a capability. When your prototype can produce real output, audio, data, a file, it stops being a demo and starts being a product argument. That is a completely different conversation to have with a client or a team. UnBeatable is proof that Figma Make can go further than most people think. What you build in the prototype layer does not have to stay there? P.S. If you want to see the full build, interaction system, synthesis engine, and AI integration, here’s the free domain link: https://lnkd.in/gUP_WvyY

webPageMetadata.title: ssshtill — Find calm and connection through touch
webPageMetadata.description: Experience soothing relief from pain, anxiety, and sleeplessness with rhythmic haptic feedback, calming audio, and gentle visuals tailored for relaxation.

content.text: We built ssshtill because the world is losing touch. Not metaphorically. Literally.
We reverse-engineered ssshtill from the most human starting point — the feeling of touch.

Solo mode uses haptic pulses and ambient visuals for anxiety, pain, and sleep. 

Shared mode connects two devices in real time through synchronized breath, touch, and rhythm.
Figma Make let us prototype touch, timing, and sync as core mechanics — not afterthoughts. Emotion became interaction.
NOTE: Haptic is not available on iOS web browsers. ssshtill is best experienced on mobile devices.

webPageMetadata.description: Easily create and manage personalized stamp collections with a user-friendly interface, perfect for book lovers wanting to share their unique stories.

content.text: I collect stamps. But cutting them out of packaging means destroying the original.
So I built Figmatelia — a hole-punch tool for photos. Upload any image, drag it under the punch, press down. The stamp appears.

The idea started as a sketch, became a Figma layout, and turned into working code through prompt refinement with Claude. The trickiest part: making the punch animation actually cut the right shape from the right coordinates. Harder than it sounds.
Built entirely with Figma Make for the .

content.text: Social platforms are flooded with bots. The "Like" is now a fake, scripted signal. I built Sapiens for the #FigmaMakeathon to prove that humanity is the only verified currency.

The Solution: Proof of Soul
Using Figma Make, I engineered a "Human Firewall":
The SAP (Emotional Resonance): No more cold clicks. Interact by showing your true self via real-time facial expressions or body gestures.
Gestural Stories: Reimagined "Story Likes." You must hand-draw a heart, an analog signature no script can replicate. ❤️
Human-Only Dessert: Hidden "Easter Egg" games inside the progress bar. Playful challenges that only a real person can appreciate. 🎮

Let's reclaim the internet for Sapiens. Leave a Like to support! ❤️

content.text: -- because there's always things we cannot control in life. Within just a few hours using Figma Make I was able to spin up an app to instantly create a calming meditation practice through the one thing we can control: our breath.

I wanted to create something that was immediately usable and had zero learning curve so anyone could pick it up and chill-the-F-out in a matter of seconds.

Features:

-  animated color theme and particles
-  animated breath pacer for inhale & exhale cycles
-  ambient background music
-  optional affirmations
60 versions, 5 breath modes, 5 dark & light color themes, 5 soundscapes, and 80 affirmations later, there's a v1.0 Instacalm app. 😌

A competitive web game where your microphone is the controller. You need to discover the shape-sound sequences encrypted on each level across 3 difficulty tiers.
-Set up in seconds: your name and a voice-generated vector avatar. Simply dictate musical notes to shape your personal polygon profile.
-Select the level you want to play.

-Start playing. Record your voice and try to guess the shapes encrypted behind every tone. Explore a range of tones until your guess corresponds to the hidden shape. The counter is ticking. The faster you guess, the better your score.

What happens when checkout becomes an experience instead of another form?

This concepts reimagines checkout as a digital try-on moment inspired by the physical retail experience. Instead of adding items to an invisible cart, products move through a conveyor and you get styled in real time, turning purchase into something you can actually see before committing.
The goal wasn’t to add friction, but to reconnect decisions with the experience and make checkout feel less like clicking and more like choosing.

Grab the Bears reimagines tap and swipe as physical gestures. Players use their right hand to grab floating bears in real time.

• Camera-driven embodied interaction

• Animated UI + sound micro-interactions

content.text: Imagine conducting ambient music in mid-air. It's like Minority Report, but instead of solving crimes you’re just gently adjusting your nervous system.
For #FigmaMakeathon I built Airwwave (AR) in #FigmaMake: a fullscreen performance instrument where gestures become live ambient soundscapes. It’s built to feel like conducting, not clicking.
Video is long because it’s a performance piece. I’m still hunting bugs, improving responsiveness, and cross browser performance until deadline.
Features: 28 ambient loops (hosted on Cloudinary), 12s fade in/out, 7-step LCD onboarding with 0–100% hold bar, 2-hand tracking + skeleton, 28 pads.
Gestures/FX: select + trigger, speed, reverb, octave, lock, stereo width + pan, tremolo/echo motion, filter resonance, volume shaping.

webPageMetadata.title: I built a drum machine with no audio files. It exports your beat, here's how: For the Figma Makeathon at Contra, I built: UnBeatable, an AI-powered drum machine prototype using Figma Make. A… | Ismel Figueroa
webPageMetadata.description:
I built a drum machine with no audio files. It exports your beat, here’s how: For the Figma Makeathon at Contra, I built: UnBeatable, an AI-powered drum machine prototype using Figma Make. A working music creation tool. Here is what makes it different from every other “interactive prototype” you have seen. Most prototypes stop at the interaction. Tap a button. Something happens. You leave with nothing. UnBeatable changes that...you leave with your downloadable beat. Here’s 7 things I built into his project: - Fully synthesized sound engine, no audio files.
- Glitch-free playback A dual setInterval and request
- Audio export Render your pattern and export your beat.
- AI beat generation using Claude/Supabase and API Credits.
- Full dark and light mode with smooth 0.25s palette transitions.
- 9 genre presets like Rock, Hip Hop, Electronic, Reggae, Latin, etc.
- Every control a real musician would expect, built into the prototype layer. Most designers prototype to show a concept. The best designers prototype to ship a capability. When your prototype can produce real output, audio, data, a file, it stops being a demo and starts being a product argument. That is a completely different conversation to have with a client or a team. UnBeatable is proof that Figma Make can go further than most people think. What you build in the prototype layer does not have to stay there? P.S. If you want to see the full build, interaction system, synthesis engine, and AI integration, here’s the free domain link: https://lnkd.in/gUP_WvyY

webPageMetadata.title: ssshtill — Find calm and connection through touch
webPageMetadata.description: Experience soothing relief from pain, anxiety, and sleeplessness with rhythmic haptic feedback, calming audio, and gentle visuals tailored for relaxation.

content.text: We built ssshtill because the world is losing touch. Not metaphorically. Literally.
We reverse-engineered ssshtill from the most human starting point — the feeling of touch.

Solo mode uses haptic pulses and ambient visuals for anxiety, pain, and sleep. 

Shared mode connects two devices in real time through synchronized breath, touch, and rhythm.
Figma Make let us prototype touch, timing, and sync as core mechanics — not afterthoughts. Emotion became interaction.
NOTE: Haptic is not available on iOS web browsers. ssshtill is best experienced on mobile devices.

webPageMetadata.description: Easily create and manage personalized stamp collections with a user-friendly interface, perfect for book lovers wanting to share their unique stories.

content.text: I collect stamps. But cutting them out of packaging means destroying the original.
So I built Figmatelia — a hole-punch tool for photos. Upload any image, drag it under the punch, press down. The stamp appears.

The idea started as a sketch, became a Figma layout, and turned into working code through prompt refinement with Claude. The trickiest part: making the punch animation actually cut the right shape from the right coordinates. Harder than it sounds.
Built entirely with Figma Make for the .

content.text: Social platforms are flooded with bots. The "Like" is now a fake, scripted signal. I built Sapiens for the #FigmaMakeathon to prove that humanity is the only verified currency.

The Solution: Proof of Soul
Using Figma Make, I engineered a "Human Firewall":
The SAP (Emotional Resonance): No more cold clicks. Interact by showing your true self via real-time facial expressions or body gestures.
Gestural Stories: Reimagined "Story Likes." You must hand-draw a heart, an analog signature no script can replicate. ❤️
Human-Only Dessert: Hidden "Easter Egg" games inside the progress bar. Playful challenges that only a real person can appreciate. 🎮

Let's reclaim the internet for Sapiens. Leave a Like to support! ❤️

webPageMetadata.description: Enhance your mindfulness with a breathing app that guides you through calming meditation practice, promoting relaxation and focus.

content.text: -- because there's always things we cannot control in life. Within just a few hours using Figma Make I was able to spin up an app to instantly create a calming meditation practice through the one thing we can control: our breath.

I wanted to create something that was immediately usable and had zero learning curve so anyone could pick it up and chill-the-F-out in a matter of seconds.

Features:

-  animated color theme and particles
-  animated breath pacer for inhale & exhale cycles
-  ambient background music
-  optional affirmations
60 versions, 5 breath modes, 5 dark & light color themes, 5 soundscapes, and 80 affirmations later, there's a v1.0 Instacalm app. 😌

A competitive web game where your microphone is the controller. You need to discover the shape-sound sequences encrypted on each level across 3 difficulty tiers.
-Set up in seconds: your name and a voice-generated vector avatar. Simply dictate musical notes to shape your personal polygon profile.
-Select the level you want to play.

-Start playing. Record your voice and try to guess the shapes encrypted behind every tone. Explore a range of tones until your guess corresponds to the hidden shape. The counter is ticking. The faster you guess, the better your score.

What happens when checkout becomes an experience instead of another form?

This concepts reimagines checkout as a digital try-on moment inspired by the physical retail experience. Instead of adding items to an invisible cart, products move through a conveyor and you get styled in real time, turning purchase into something you can actually see before committing.
The goal wasn’t to add friction, but to reconnect decisions with the experience and make checkout feel less like clicking and more like choosing.

webPageMetadata.description: Connect with next-gen talent and tools to get work underway. Hire more independents. Start more projects. Get more creative.

content.text: Really excited to be growing on Contra. 🚀
I've always loved platforms where creators can turn ideas into reality, showcase their imagination, and build something people genuinely enjoy using.
That's why I decided to join the
Config Makeathon and create something ambitious:
🐻 Bearify — an all-in-one companion app designed to simplify everyday life.
Instead of juggling multiple apps, Bearify brings everything together in one place:

• 📔 Personal Diary
• ⏰ Reminders
• 💪 Exercise Tracking
• ✨ Daily Quotes & Motivation
• 📚 Articles & Knowledge Hub
• 🎮 Interactive Pet Gaming
• 💬 Chat with Your Pet
• 🛍️ Shop Features
• ✅ Task Management
• 🛒 Grocery Planning
• ...and much more.
The goal was simple: create one app that helps users stay productive, motivated, organized, and entertained without constantly switching between different platforms.
Building this for the Config Makeathon was an incredible experience, and I'd love to hear what you think.
If you like the project, feel free to check it out and support it:
Feedback, thoughts, and support are always appreciated. ❤️
You did an amazing job. Good luck today! Keep inspiring 'n shining 'n energising others 🔥

content.text: Day 5 — the final day of my journal posting challenge 💙
Over the past few days, I’ve shared little pieces of my life through this journal: exciting moments, anxious moments, memories, reflections, and even a very important zucchini update 🥒✨

When I started building this project, I wanted to create a space that would make self-reflection feel a little easier and a little more enjoyable. I hope both this product and my own experience have inspired at least a few people to try journaling, pay attention to their thoughts, and build a habit of reflecting on their days.
Today I’m waiting for the Makeathon results, and honestly, it feels both exciting and terrifying at the same time. 🤞
If you’d like to explore the project, try the product, or support my submission, you can find everything at the link below:
Thank you for all the likes, comments, shares, feedback, and kind words throughout this journey. Your support has meant more than you know. 💙

content.text: ⛳️ 𝗦𝗸𝗶𝗹𝗹 𝗔𝘀𝘀𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗠𝗮𝘁𝗿𝗶𝘅 & 𝗥𝗼𝗮𝗱𝗺𝗮𝗽 𝗕𝘂𝗶𝗹𝗱𝗲𝗿 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗗𝗲𝘀𝗶𝗴𝗻𝗲𝗿𝘀

Skill Assessment Matrix is a SaaS designed to help Product Designers and UI/UX Designers better understand their current level, identify their strongest skills, and build a personalized growth roadmap
The idea came from a problem I experienced early in my career:
"𝙃𝙤𝙬 𝙙𝙤 𝙄 𝙠𝙣𝙤𝙬 𝙞𝙛 𝙄'𝙢 𝙖 𝙅𝙪𝙣𝙞𝙤𝙧, 𝙈𝙞𝙙-𝙡𝙚𝙫𝙚𝙡, 𝙤𝙧 𝙎𝙚𝙣𝙞𝙤𝙧 𝙙𝙚𝙨𝙞𝙜𝙣𝙚𝙧?"
This first version is focused on the desktop experience and was designed as the foundation for a larger SaaS product

𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗕𝗟𝗘𝗠
Designers often struggle with:
✦ Understanding their current level
✦ Identifying their strongest skills
✦ Knowing what to improve next
✦ Creating a clear career growth plan

𝗦𝗢𝗟𝗨𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡
Skill Assessment Matrix helps designers answer three simple questions:
✦ Where am I today?
✦ What are my strongest skills?
✦ What should I focus on next?
The product guides users through a complete career planning flow:
𝗦𝘁𝗲𝗽 𝟭 [Career Goals Setup] Users define their career goals and desired direction
𝗦𝘁𝗲𝗽 𝟮 [Self Assessment] Users evaluate their current skills across multiple competency areas
𝗦𝘁𝗲𝗽 𝟯 [Summary] The system generates a personalized overview highlighting strengths and growth opportunities.
𝗦𝘁𝗲𝗽 𝟰 [Roadmap Builder] Users receive a personalized roadmap and can further customize it by adding their own skills
𝗦𝘁𝗲𝗽 𝟱 [PDF Export] The final roadmap can be exported as a PDF and used as a practical career development plan.
⚡️This roadmap generation and export flow became one of the most valuable features of the project⚡️

𝗥𝗘𝗦𝗘𝗔𝗥𝗖𝗛 𝗙𝗢𝗨𝗡𝗗𝗔𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡
This project is based on more than 5 years of research into Skill assessment frameworks. The primary foundation for this work was the Figma Community Skills Matrix documentation, supported by dozens of additional public frameworks

𝗪𝗛𝗔𝗧 𝗪𝗔𝗦 𝗧𝗘𝗦𝗧𝗘𝗗
✦ Figma AI Agent for generating new interfaces
✦ Figma Make for transforming designs into interactive product flows
✦ Combining a custom design system with generated product experiences
✦ Building complex multi-step user journeys inside Figma Make
✦ Creating SaaS-style product logic without traditional development workflows

content.text: In a busy world filled with work, responsibilities, and constant distractions, many people lose touch with their emotions, needs, and personal goals
While journaling can improve self-awareness and well-being, traditional journals are not always practical to use every day
I created Digital Journal that makes self-reflection simple and accessible

💎 Track mood and energy
💎 Reflect on daily experiences
💎 Practice gratitude
💎 Capture thoughts, memories, and dreams
💎 Build greater self-awareness over time
The goal was to combine the convenience of digital tools with the warmth of a scrapbook-style journal
During the Makeathon, I explored and tested:

✔️Claude + Figma MCP for generating initial wireframes
✔️Figma AI Agent for early concept exploration and wireframing
✔️Figma for core pages visual design
✔️Figma Make for building and testing the interactive prototype
🎥 Watch the demo video to see the full design process, tools used, and how the project was built from idea to interactive prototype
If you enjoyed this project, I would appreciate your support ❤️
